title: Get Involved
text:
Student Research Opportunities
PRISE Lab offers hands-on research experience for undergraduate and graduate students at UT Dallas. No prior research experience is required — just genuine interest and reliability. We mentor students through every step of the research process.
Interested? Apply here.

title: PRISE Lab
text:
About
We are a research hub at UT Dallas dedicated to understanding and solving critical challenges in environmental sustainability, energy, and economic inequality. We focus on the human impact of environmental and economic shocks, particularly how they affect marginalized populations.
Our research spans hurricane impacts in the U.S., tropical forest losses, energy access in sub-Saharan Africa, and pollution effects worldwide.

title: PRISE Lab
text:
Research Areas

Climate and Political Accountability
How extreme weather events shape election outcomes and climate policy.


Conservation Program Effectiveness
Optimizing conservation funding — evidence from Vietnam and beyond.


Clean Energy Adoption
Household adoption of energy-efficient cooking appliances in Uganda.


Land-Use Change and Deforestation
Political economy of deforestation in Brazil and Indonesia using remote sensing and causal analysis.

title: Team
text:
Faculty




Dr. Erin Litzow
Applied economist
Environmental, development, and energy economics. Research across 11 countries in Africa and Latin America. Affiliated with Duke Energy Access Project and Environment for Development.
Website · erin.litzow@utdallas.edu





Dr. Elías Cisneros
Environmental economist
Political economy of land-use change and deforestation in Brazil and Indonesia. Remote sensing and causal analysis methods.
Website · elias.cisneros@utdallas.edu

title: Team

PhD candidate, Public Policy & Political Economy
Climate variability, human capital, and political economy. Research on extreme heat and educational outcomes, weather shocks in India, and patent reforms in emerging markets. UT System Graduate Archer Fellow and NSF-funded EITM Scholar.

title: Campus Drinking Water Quality Study
text:
We are assembling a student team to test drinking water quality across campus for PFAS, lead, and other contaminants, survey the campus community on willingness to pay for improvements, and conduct a cost-benefit analysis of treatment options. We need students from three areas:

Environmental Science — field sampling, lab coordination, data interpretation
Social Science / Economics / Public Policy — survey design, econometric analysis
Engineering — water treatment costing, cost-benefit analysis

To apply, email erin.litzow@utdallas.edu and elias.cisneros@utdallas.edu with your name, major, year, which team interests you, and why.

title: News
text:
April 2026 - PhD student Yiqun Yu presents his research linking temperature change and other climate shocks to learning outcomes in East Africa at the 4th Annual Texas Applied Microeconomics Student (TEAMS) workshop at Rice University.
April 2026 - Undergraduate student Joey Rogers presents his research “When the River Runs Dry: Drought and Deforestation in Vietnam Forest Policy” at UT-D’s Undergraduate Research Scholar Awards Poster Contest Symposium.
April 2026 — PRISE Lab seeking students to apply for UTD Green Fund campus drinking water quality study. Learn more if interested.
April 2026 — PRISE Lab website goes live.
